prompt: Write a warm greeting to a new colleague in five different languages (your choice). After each, add a one-line note about what's culturally implied by the register you chose.
response:
1) English: Welcome to the team — we’re so glad to have you! (warm, friendly workplace greeting)  
   Note: Casual and open; implies approachability and readiness to collaborate.

2) Spanish: ¡Bienvenido/a al equipo! Estamos muy contentos de que te unas a nosotros. (Welcome to the team! We’re very happy you’re joining us.)  
   Note: Using "bienvenido/a" and "te" (tú) is warm and informal — adjust gender and use "usted" for more formality in some regions or hierarchies.

3) French: Bienvenue dans l'équipe ! Nous sommes ravis de vous compter parmi nous. (Welcome to the team! We’re delighted to have you with us.)  
   Note: The use of "vous" is polite and respectful; it conveys professional warmth while keeping formal distance.

4) Japanese: チームへようこそ。これからよろしくお願いします。 (Chiimu e youkoso. Kore kara yoroshiku onegaishimasu.)  
   Note: Polite and standard for workplaces; "yoroshiku onegaishimasu" expresses humility and an expectation of good, ongoing cooperation.

5) Mandarin Chinese: 欢迎加入我们的团队！很高兴与你共事。 (Huānyíng jiārù wǒmen de tuánduì! Hěn gāoxìng yǔ nǐ gòngshì.)  
   Note: Using "你" is friendly and collegial; for extra formality or to show respect to seniors, people may use "您" instead.
